title: Resolve 6 Dependabot alerts: undici <7.24.0 (transitive via miniflare) in _cloudflare/r2
description:
GitHub Dependabot has flagged 6 vulnerabilities against `undici` in `_cloudflare/r2/pnpm-lock.yaml`. All 6 advisories share the same vulnerable range (`>=7.0.0, <7.24.0`) and same patched version (`7.24.0`), so they collapse to one fix decision and one upgrade.

Currently locked: `undici@7.18.2`, pulled in transitively as `wrangler@4.59.1` → `miniflare@4.20260128.0` → `undici@7.18.2`. Not a direct dependency.

## Alerts

| # | Severity | CVE | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| [#39](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/39) | high | CVE-2026-1526 | Unbounded Memory Consumption in WebSocket permessage-deflate Decompression |
| [#38](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/38) | high | CVE-2026-2229 | Unhandled Exception in WebSocket Client (invalid `server_max_window_bits`) |
| [#35](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/35) | high | CVE-2026-1528 | Malicious WebSocket 64-bit length overflow crashes client |
| [#37](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/37) | medium | CVE-2026-1527 | CRLF Injection via `upgrade` option |
| [#36](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/36) | medium | CVE-2026-2581 | Unbounded Memory Consumption in `DeduplicationHandler` (DoS) |
| [#34](https://github.com/alexmensch/alxm.me/security/dependabot/34) | medium | CVE-2026-1525 | HTTP Request/Response Smuggling |

## Decision needed

**Fix or dismiss?** Reachability is low — `undici` here is only used by `miniflare` for local Workers emulation during `wrangler` operations in the R2 sync script (`_cloudflare/r2/`). The script does not run a long-lived server, does not accept untrusted WebSocket connections, and only runs in CI/dev to push files to R2. Most of the high-severity advisories require attacker-controlled WebSocket traffic, which we don't expose. So strict exploitability here is near zero.

That said, dismissing 6 alerts (3 high, 3 medium) is noisier than just bumping. Recommendation: **fix**, because the upgrade is cheap.

## Fix options

1. **Bump `wrangler`** (`4.59.1` → `4.87.0`). Latest `miniflare@4.20260430.0` ships `undici@7.24.8` transitively. Cleanest — root-cause fix, no override drift, picks up other miniflare/wrangler fixes too. Risk: minor wrangler API changes; verify `_cloudflare/r2/` scripts still work end-to-end.
2. **`pnpm.overrides`** (add `"undici": ">=7.24.0"` next to existing `fast-xml-parser` override in `_cloudflare/r2/package.json`). Surgical, isolates the change to one transitive package. Use if option 1 introduces wrangler regressions.

Prefer option 1; fall back to option 2 if wrangler bump misbehaves.

## Cross-repo

Sister repo `alexmensch/alexmarshall.me` has the identical 6 alerts at the same `_cloudflare/r2/pnpm-lock.yaml` and same `wrangler` version. Whichever fix is chosen here should be mirrored there in the same PR cycle. Tracking only here per "do the work once" guidance — alexmarshall.me has its own beads instance so a separate issue there would just duplicate this one.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 6 Dependabot alerts on alxm.me are resolved (auto-closed by Dependabot once `pnpm-lock.yaml` reflects `undici >=7.24.0`)
- [ ] R2 sync (`pnpm run build:cf` or equivalent) still succeeds with the upgraded dependencies
- [ ] Same fix applied to `alexmensch/alexmarshall.me`

title: titleCaseTag helper + per-tag Atom feeds
description: (1) Add titleCaseTag function to src/_data/helpers.js — splits hyphenated tag (e.g. personal-growth) on hyphens and title-cases each word; registered as a Liquid filter. Frontmatter raw value stays lowercase. (2) New src/writing-tag-feeds.liquid + src/writing-tag-feeds.11tydata.js: paginates over unique tags, generates /writing/{tag-slug}.atom for each. Same Atom 1.0 entry format as the existing /writing.atom — extracted to shared partial src/_includes/partials/atom-entry.liquid (used by both feeds). Feed metadata reflects the tag name. Existing /writing.atom unchanged (still includes all writing articles).

title: Per-tag phrase mapping (src/_data/tagPhrases.js)
description: New src/_data/tagPhrases.js — an object mapping tag string to phrase template (e.g. psychology → 'From the annals of {tag}'). Entries already populated for the existing tags in the writing corpus: art, aviation, essay, fiction, financial, nature, software, plus psychology. Validation plugin enforces every tag has an entry; build fails otherwise (see tag-validation task).

title: New Yorker–style article flourish (with linked tag)
description: On individual article pages, render an editorial category flourish using a per-tag phrase mapping (e.g. 'From the annals of Psychology'). The tag name within the phrase is a link to /writing/{tag-slug}/. Modify src/_includes/layouts/posts.liquid and src/_includes/partials/article.liquid. New tagPhrase filter looks up the phrase template and substitutes a {tag} placeholder with the title-cased linked tag. Flourish uses the article's first tag only. New .article__flourish styles in src/assets/scss/blocks/_article.scss: sans-serif, small, uppercase, letter-spaced, visually secondary.

title: Tag archive pages at /writing/{tag-slug}/
description: New src/writing/tags.liquid + src/writing/tags.11tydata.js. Paginates over unique tags from the writing collection (using shared helpers.getUniqueTags()). Generates /writing/{tag-slug}/ for every tag that exists on a published article. Uses layouts/writing.liquid; hero title 'Writing: {Tag Name}' (title-cased). Filtering by array containment (tags.includes(tag)) using a Liquid loop with 'contains'. Tag slug uses helpers.toSlug (URL-safe lowercase hyphenated).

title: Pills link to tag archive pages
description: Each category pill on the writing collection list is a native anchor element to /writing/{tag-slug}/, matching the archive page route from the related task. Pill is a standard <a>, keyboard-focusable. Uses the toSlug filter (registered in .eleventy.js — exposes existing helpers.toSlug as a Liquid filter).

title: Curated tag colour palette + tagColorIndex helper (light + dark mode)
description: In src/assets/scss/global/_variables.scss, add 8–12 --tag-color-N CSS custom properties at :root and override in @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark). Derive from existing colour families. Each colour meets WCAG AA 3:1 contrast against its mode's background. Add tagColorIndex helper to src/_data/helpers.js (simple string hash modulo TAG_PALETTE_SIZE) and register as a Liquid filter. Same tag → same colour. Cross-reference comment on TAG_PALETTE_SIZE pointing to _variables.scss.

title: Category pill on writing collection list
description: Modify src/_includes/partials/writing-collection.liquid: add pill <a> next to the date for each article entry. Each pill shows the article's first tag in title case, sans-serif font, thin outline (no fill) using inline style --pill-color CSS var. New SCSS block src/assets/scss/blocks/_category-pill.scss with border-radius: 100vw, border: 1px solid var(--pill-color), small font size, max-width: 20ch with text-overflow: ellipsis to handle long tag names. Pill is keyboard-accessible.

title: Tag validation plugin + tagPhrases enforcement
description: New eleventy-plugins/tag-validation.js (following audio-validation.js pattern). Exports testable validateWritingTags(collection, tagPhrases). Each article in writing collection must have at least one tag (frontmatter 'tags' array). Every tag used in published articles must have a corresponding entry in src/_data/tagPhrases.js. Build fails with a clear, file-named error otherwise. Validation runs after KV plugin populates the collection (eleventy.after / addCollection callback). Validation is invoked from src/writing/tags.11tydata.js (no plugin wrapper).

title: Add category indicators to writing collection (PR #211)
description:
Surface article categories throughout the writing section: pill-shaped tag labels on the collection list (linking to per-tag archive pages), per-tag Atom feeds, and a New Yorker–style editorial flourish on individual article pages. Build-time validation enforces every article has at least one tag and every tag has a phrase mapping.

Source: previously documented in product/category-indicators.md (now removed). Implementation lives on branch claude/add-category-indicators-6mStC — see PR #211 for review.

Out-of-spec changes already made on the branch: shared getUniqueTags helper, shared atom-entry partial, multi-tag support (single tag → tags array), frontmatter rename to standard 'tags' field, and tagPhrases entries for art/aviation/essay/fiction/financial/nature/software.

Files added: eleventy-plugins/tag-validation.js, src/_data/tagPhrases.js, src/assets/scss/blocks/_category-pill.scss, src/writing/tags.{liquid,11tydata.js}, src/writing-tag-feeds.{liquid,11tydata.js}, src/_includes/partials/atom-entry.liquid.

Files modified: .eleventy.js, src/_data/helpers.js, src/_includes/partials/writing-collection.liquid, src/_includes/layouts/posts.liquid, src/_includes/partials/article.liquid, src/assets/scss/global/_variables.scss, src/assets/scss/blocks/_article.scss, src/assets/scss/blocks/_index.scss, src/writing/writing.md, src/writing-atom-feed.liquid.

title: Add switcher-2 composition class + apply to contact options
description: In src/assets/scss/compositions/_switcher.scss, add a .switcher-2 class that uses the existing switcher mixin with $max-items: 2 and the same $min-item-width as .switcher (dt.get-value('measure', 'compact')). Then in src/_includes/partials/contact-options.liquid, change the container class from [ switcher ] to [ switcher-2 ] so the 4 option cards render 2x2 on wide viewports and stack on narrow. Existing .switcher (3-item) stays unchanged.

title: Style subscribe form within contact card + override ghost button
description: In src/assets/scss/blocks/_contact-options.scss, add scoped styles for the subscribe form when nested in a contact-options card: no redundant box/border/background, button uses secondary background (override ghost variant) to match other CTA links. Specificity scoped to .contact-options .subscribe-form so article-page styling is unaffected.

title: Reduce subscribe form weight and soften CTA copy
description:
In src/_includes/partials/subscribe-form.liquid, drop weight-extra-bold from the CTA

element (keep [ font-sans ]). Update CTA text in article.liquid from 'Want new posts in your inbox?' to 'Enjoyed this? Get new posts by email.', and update the partial's default fallback assignment to match.

title: Add subscribe card to contact page (with site data threading)
description: Add a fourth options[] entry to src/contact.md front matter with type: subscribe and a body field. Update contact-options.liquid to detect option.type == 'subscribe' and render the subscribe-form partial in place of a link CTA. Thread site through layouts/contact.liquid → article.liquid → contact-options.liquid → subscribe-form.liquid so the form has access to site.newsletter.apiUrl and site.newsletter.channelId. Add compact: true parameter to subscribe-form.liquid that omits box/bg/padding/border utilities for in-card embed.

title: Move subscribe form below article content
description: In src/_includes/partials/article.liquid, move the {% render "partials/subscribe-form" %} block from before {{ content }} to after it, still inside .article__content and gated by {% if subscription == true %}. The episode media player (type == 'episode') continues to render before content. Acceptance: subscribe form HTML appears after article body; subscription guard preserved; short content still shows form below.

title: Improve subscribe form UX (PR #208)
description:
Reposition the email subscribe form below article content (instead of above), reduce its visual weight, soften CTA copy, and add a subscribe option card to the contact page. Also introduces a switcher-2 CSS composition for the resulting 2x2 contact-options layout.

Source: previously documented in product/improve-subscribe-form-ux.md (now removed). Implementation lives on branch claude/improve-subscribe-form-ux-i5xYE — see PR #208 for review.

All CSS changes go through the cube-css skill (CUBE CSS conventions, design tokens).

Files touched: src/_includes/partials/article.liquid, subscribe-form.liquid, contact-options.liquid; src/_includes/layouts/contact.liquid; src/contact.md; src/assets/scss/blocks/_contact-options.scss; src/assets/scss/compositions/_switcher.scss.

title: Add pnpm test to build pipeline
description: Update build script to: 'pnpm run format && pnpm run lint && pnpm test && npx @11ty/eleventy && node check-permalinks.js'. Tests run before Eleventy build so failures abort early. Acceptance: pnpm test exits non-zero on failure and aborts the build.

title: Delete src/about.md
description: After the redirect lands, delete src/about.md so no duplicate content exists at both / and /about/. Acceptance: src/about.md does not exist; build completes; no /about/index.html generated.

title: 301 redirect /about/ → /
description: Add a permanent redirect to the existing _redirects file so /about/ returns 301 to /. Preserves SEO equity and prevents broken external links. Acceptance: GET /about/ returns 301 with Location: /; no redirect loop (/ does not redirect).

title: Remove About from site navigation
description: Remove the 'About' nav entry from src/_data/site.js (or wherever nav is configured). It must not appear in header nav or footer nav. No replacement link added. Acceptance: rendered header has no /about/ link; rendered footer has no /about/ link; nav item count drops by one in both; other items (Counselling, Writing, Artwork, Podcast, Contact) unchanged.

title: Conditional hero rendering in article partial
description: Article partial only renders the hero section when 'hero' front matter is present. Pages without hero data emit no hero wrapper markup. Acceptance: a landing-layout page without hero front matter has no .hero element in rendered HTML; existing pages with hero front matter unchanged.

title: Promote About content to homepage at /
description: Current About page content (body text, blockQuote, all sections) becomes the new homepage at /. Uses the landing layout, shows site header and footer, no hero rendered. <title> is 'Home' (or site default), not 'About me'. Acceptance: GET / returns full About body content with chrome; no .hero element rendered; blockQuote shortcode and markdown render correctly; internal links (/coaching, /contact) still work.

title: Exclude /hello/ from search indexing (noindex)
description: Add <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> to the /hello/ page <head>. Acceptance: rendered HTML at /hello/ contains the noindex directive in <head>; other pages unaffected.

title: Relocate current homepage to /hello/
description: Current minimal landing page (title, intro text, links) moves from / to /hello/. Retains its existing layout/content/style — no header, no footer. Not listed in any site nav. Acceptance: GET /hello/ returns the current landing page content, no header/footer, no nav listing in either header or footer.

title: Comprehensive test suite for pure functions, worker handlers, and build utilities
description:
Expand test coverage from the original 27 tests (LLM opt-out only) into a full suite covering helper functions, podcast/opengraph data, worker request handlers (R2, RSS, MIME), permalink diff logic, audio validation, custom shortcodes, and markdown processing. All tests in tests/ using node:test + assert/strict.

Source: previously documented in product/test-suite.md (now removed).

Status: shipped — tests/ contains 14 test files, worker.js exports its handlers, and src/_build/ has the extracted shortcodes and markdown modules. Re-open this epic or specific child tasks if coverage gaps are discovered against the original spec (see git history at the time of migration for the original requirement detail).

title: LLM training opt-out signals (TDM-Reservation, noimageai, llms.txt policy)
description:
Strengthen the site's machine-readable signals that content may not be used for AI model training, while preserving permission for search indexing and AI input (RAG/grounding/citations).

Three layers:
1. TDM-Reservation: 1 HTTP header on all HTML responses (worker/index.js)
2. <meta name="robots" content="noimageai"> on all pages (src/_includes/partials/site-meta.liquid)
3. Usage Policy section in src/llms.txt.liquid aligned with robots.txt Content-Signal (search=yes, ai-input=yes, ai-train=no)

Source: previously documented in product/llm-training-opt-out.md (now removed).

Status: shipped — all three signals are present in the rendered site.

title: Homepage restructure: promote About content, relocate /hello/
description:
Promote substantive personal/professional content (currently the About page) to the homepage to strengthen SEO. Relocate the existing minimal landing page to /hello/ for use as a social media link-in-bio (no header/footer, noindex, not in nav).

Source: previously documented in product/homepage-restructure.md (now removed; this issue and its children are the canonical source of truth).

Acceptance: requests to / serve the promoted content with site chrome; /hello/ serves the link-in-bio page sans chrome and is excluded from search; /about/ 301-redirects to /; no duplicate content.

Out of scope: content edits to the promoted homepage, redesign of /hello/, new SEO meta beyond what About already had, changes to layouts/home, permalink baseline updates (mechanical).

value:
Prefer small topical commits: when a change touches multiple concerns, split them into separate commits each with a focused subject line and brief why-body.

Why: validated previously — splitting unrelated work into separate commits was accepted without pushback. One-concept-per-commit keeps the repo's story legible and makes revert/bisect surgical.

How to apply: when there are unrelated staged changes, don't reach for one omnibus commit message — stage and commit each concern separately. Err toward more commits, not fewer. Keep using HEREDOC commit format with the Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer.

Concurrent Claude sessions: bd issues marked in_progress with matching uncommitted edits in the worktree usually indicate another live session is working on that bead. Do NOT pick it up, run quality gates against it, stage/commit/push, or close the bead. Confirm with the user first. The Status: in_progress field is the canonical 'someone else has this' signal — git branch ownership alone is not enough because branches are shared across sessions.

Don't rm -rf _site during a live dev preview. Why: it kills the running 11ty dev server's served assets, breaks any browser tabs the user has open against the preview, and forces a cold rebuild. How to apply: if a stale build is suspected during 'pnpm run 11ty:watch', stop the watcher first (or 'pnpm run clean' between runs, which is fine before starting the watcher) — never delete _site while the watcher is live. Same applies if any other dev preview is serving files out of _site/.

Don't loop on failed web fetches. If a URL access fails (404, blocked, auth wall, AI-scraper filter, etc.), do NOT keep trying alternative transports (WebFetch -> curl -> different URL -> etc.) in a loop.

Why: the user flagged this explicitly — they don't want time and tokens burned on transport attempts that aren't converging. It's faster for them to grab it manually than to thrash.

How to apply: on the first failure, try one alternative. On the second failure, stop and ask the user to fetch the content themselves and paste it in (or drop the file into the repo, or give the information directly). Tell them the exact URL being attempted so they can fetch it with minimal context-switching.

How to author and maintain bd content well across sessions.

(1) Prefer formal bd dep links over prose cross-references whenever the relationship affects planning, ordering, or future work decisions. Alex plans from the dependency graph (bd ready, bd dep list, bd show) — prose 'see issue X' references don't surface there and are easy to miss. Use 'bd dep add' for blocks, the relate variant for relates_to, --parent for parent-child. After filing a batch of issues, audit own descriptions for cross-reference prose and convert each to a formal link before declaring done. Use 'relate' for cross-epic connections (bd's blocks between epics often errors). Avoid epic-level blocks when task-level deps can express the same constraint, since open parents with inbound blockers cause bd ready to cascade-block all children.

(2) When you notice a bd memory is stale, deficient, or contradicts current repo state, update it via 'bd remember --key <existing-key> ...' immediately rather than just noting it in chat. Why: Alex wants memories actively maintained — drift compounds across sessions if corrections aren't written back, and the next session reads the stale version. How to apply: when reading a memory whose specifics don't match current code/config (referenced file no longer exists, flag renamed, policy changed, commit hash referenced is gone, etc.), re-author the memory body in place. Rewrite the whole body each time so it stays self-contained; fragmented amendments are hard to read. Same applies to memories about commands, paths, and project structure.

bd create --deps='blocks:OTHER' direction trap: --deps='blocks:X' on bd create makes the NEW issue block X, not the other way around — opposite of what 'depends' usually means. Workarounds: (1) use plain id with no prefix in --deps (the 'or id' form treats it as 'this issue depends on'), or (2) skip --deps and run 'bd dep <blocker> --blocks <blocked>' after creation. Always verify direction with 'bd dep list <new-issue>' and 'bd ready' immediately after.

How bd state persists in this project — two related operational facts:

(1) bd state is shared across all git worktrees. Running bd commands from a .claude/worktrees/* checkout writes to the same database the main checkout reads from. The .beads/issues.jsonl file in any worktree is a stale snapshot from worktree creation, NOT the live source of truth — don't be alarmed when it doesn't change after bd writes; don't redo bd commands; don't go searching for DB paths. Trust bd's exit code: success means persisted globally.

(2) 'bd dolt push' is a no-op here — no Dolt remote is configured (the embedded dolt at .beads/embeddeddolt has no upstream; verify with 'bd dolt remote list'). The git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl is the source of truth and propagates via normal 'git push'. Skip 'bd dolt push' in the session-close workflow for this project; do not treat its informational 'Supported remote URLs' output as needing fixing. The CLAUDE.md project workflow lists 'bd dolt push' generically — that's fine to leave as documentation of the standard pattern, but in practice for alxm.me only 'git push' is needed for beads to propagate.

Defer doc updates until the final commit. Don't edit CLAUDE.md, README.md, or anything under product/ or docs/ while implementing a change — treat code changes as the only deliverable until commit time.

Why: mid-session doc edits become churn — direction shifts, features get dropped, parameters rename, knob values move; the paragraph written early ends up describing something that no longer exists.

How to apply: during implementation confine edits to code, templates, styles, tests; if a code comment references a doc section about to be wrong, leave it and surface at commit time. At commit time, grep the final diff for renames, removed knobs, new behavior, anything user-visible — open CLAUDE.md, README.md, product/, docs/ and update only what's now stale. Skip ones that don't need changes.

Project stack and key paths for alxm.me.

Stack:
- Eleventy static site (Liquid + Nunjucks templates)
- Deployed to Cloudflare Workers + R2 for large assets
- SCSS with CUBE CSS methodology
- Utopia fluid typography and spacing (utopia-core-scss)

Key SCSS paths (verify before relying on these — code is authoritative):
- SCSS entry: src/assets/scss/root.scss
- Compositions: src/assets/scss/compositions/
- Utilities: src/assets/scss/utilities/
- Blocks: src/assets/scss/blocks/
- Design tokens: src/assets/scss/config/_config.scss
- CSS custom properties: src/assets/scss/global/_variables.scss
- Themes (dark mode, artwork): src/assets/scss/global/_themes.scss

Related project: alexmarshall.me is a counselling-focused fork of alxm.me, created 2026-03-17.

Repo: https://github.com/alexmensch/alexmarshall.me (public)

Key differences from alxm.me (verify against current code — fork may have diverged further):
- Counselling landing page as homepage (permalink /)
- Writing filtered to psychology-tagged articles only via pagination.before in writing.11tydata.js (checks item.tags)
- Writing articles have canonical URLs pointing to alxm.me
- Podcast is a simple platform links page (no RSS/episodes/audio)
- Guided inner journey exercises at /exercises/guided-journeys/ with R2-hosted MP3s
- Removed: artwork, about, advisory, OG images, syntax highlighting, permalink tracking, audio validation, LLMs.txt, Atom feed, loremIpsum
- Simplified worker (R2 + static assets, no RSS handling)
- Company info always shown in footer (no conditional)
- SCSS cascade order fixed: compositions → utilities → blocks

Why: User wants a separate, focused counselling practice website at alexmarshall.me.

How to apply: When working on either site, be aware they share the same Cloudflare KV namespace for writing content but are otherwise independent repos.

CSS Architecture for alxm.me. Core approach: CUBE CSS + Every Layout primitives + Utopia fluid type/space.

Rules:
- NEVER hardcode values. Always stay within the design token system.
- CSS values must use CSS custom properties (--flow-space, --gutter, etc.) or SCSS helpers (dt.get-size(), dt.get-color(), dt.use(), dt.get-value()).
- Exceptions (state variations) use data-* attributes, never additional CSS classes.
- HTML class grouping: class="[ block ] [ composition ] [ utilities ]" with square brackets.
- First reach for an existing composition or utility before writing new CSS.

There is a 'cube-css' skill in this repository — invoke it via the Skill tool when making any CSS/SCSS or font changes. Read it before making changes.

SCSS cascade order rule: in src/assets/scss/root.scss, imports must be ordered compositions → utilities → blocks.

Why: Per CUBE CSS methodology, blocks refine compositions and must appear later in the cascade. PR #214 (alxm.me) fixed this — the previous order (utilities → blocks → compositions) only worked by accident because _artwork.scss transitively loaded compositions early via @use "../compositions". This caused .box { outline: transparent } to override .contact-options { outline-color } on the alexmarshall.me fork (which removed _artwork.scss).

How to apply: When making SCSS changes to either alxm.me or alexmarshall.me, ensure the cascade order in root.scss is respected: compositions before utilities before blocks. Reorder if a refactor breaks it.
